ev.headline: Global EV sales hit 1.1 million units in February 2026, market splitting by region
ev.summary: Global EV sales reached 1.1 million units in February 2026 according to Benchmark Mineral Intelligence, with sharp regional variations emerging in the market.

ev.headline: Rivian launches crucial R2 EV this spring starting at $58,000
ev.summary: Rivian unveiled its make-or-break R2 midsize electric vehicle launching spring 2026 with a Performance model at $57,990, featuring 330-mile range and advanced driver assistance.

ev.headline: Volkswagen leads China sales with 100,000+ vehicles in February, plans new EV every two weeks
ev.summary: Volkswagen sold over 100,000 vehicles in China in February, overtaking BYD and Toyota, with plans to launch a new EV every two weeks on average for 'China Speed.'

ev.headline: BYD Denza Z9 GT world's longest-range pure EV heads to Europe with 500-mile range
ev.summary: BYD's Denza Z9 GT, marketed as the world's longest-range pure EV, is heading to Europe offering nearly 500 miles of WLTP range and 5-minute fast charging capability.

ev.headline: Lucid Motors reveals Cosmos and Earth midsize EVs starting under €43,000
ev.summary: Lucid Motors announced two new midsize electric vehicles—Cosmos and Earth—launching this year on a new platform targeting drivers wanting high-performance EVs for under €43,000.

ev.headline: Automaker EV losses hit $70 billion; Honda joining massive restructuring retreat
ev.summary: Honda is scrapping three electric vehicles and booking up to $15.8 billion in losses, bringing the auto industry's cumulative EV write-downs from restructuring efforts to approximately $70 billion.

ev.headline: Ford, GM, Stellantis combined write-off $52.1 billion from EV strategy pivots
ev.summary: The Big Three Detroit automakers collectively took $52.1 billion in write-downs—Ford $19.5B, GM $6.6B, Stellantis $26B—as they retreat from aggressive EV investments and shift toward hybrids.

ev.headline: Porsche Cayenne Electric Turbo becomes most powerful production Porsche with 1,000+ hp
ev.summary: The 2026 Porsche Cayenne Electric in Turbo form will produce over 1,000 horsepower, making it the most powerful production Porsche ever with 0-60 mph in 3 seconds.

ev.headline: Mercedes CLA EV combines affordability with 800-volt charging and 400+ mile range
ev.summary: Mercedes-Benz's new CLA EV features innovative 800-volt architecture, over 400 miles of range, and 270+ horsepower while being built in Hungary to offer more affordable pricing.

ev.headline: Chevy Bolt returns as 2027 model with 2.5x faster DC charging, 255-mile range
ev.summary: Chevrolet's affordable Bolt EV returns for 2027 with GM's Ultium battery technology, DC fast-charging 2.5 times faster than the original, 255-mile range, starting under $30,000.

ev.headline: BYD overtakes Tesla as world's largest EV seller with 2.26 million units in 2025
ev.summary: Chinese automaker BYD surpassed Tesla as the world's largest EV seller, delivering 2.26 million electric vehicles in 2025, up 28% year-over-year.

ev.headline: 32 new EV models launching in US 2026, marking most exciting year for electric vehicles
ev.summary: Thirty-two new electric vehicle models are confirmed for 2026 US market launch, representing a mature market with diverse options across multiple price points and segments.

ev.headline: U.S. EV sales plunge 41% after federal tax credit expires; market faces headwinds
ev.summary: EV registrations dropped 41% and fell to just 5.2% of new vehicle sales in Q4 2025 after the $7,500 federal tax credit expired September 30, down from 10.3% peak in September.

ev.headline: CES 2026: Autel Energy launches new EV charging infrastructure with automation focus
ev.summary: At CES 2026, EV charging provider Autel Energy unveiled its Avant family of automated charging solutions for fleet depots and autonomous vehicle operations supporting open standards.

ev.headline: BMW iX3 demand 'significantly exceeds expectations' with orders extending into 2026
ev.summary: BMW reports that demand for its new iX3 electric SUV is 'significantly exceeding expectations,' with order books filled well into 2026, signaling strong market appetite.

ev.headline: Wireless EV charging emerges as mainstream 2026 trend with Porsche leading adoption
ev.summary: Wireless inductive EV charging technology becomes a genuine option rather than experiment in 2026, with Porsche's 22 kW wireless plate and automated positioning setting new standards.

ev.headline: Solid-state battery production ramps with ProLogium and Darfon partnership at CES 2026
ev.summary: ProLogium and Darfon Energy Tech introduced new solid-state battery solutions at CES 2026, advancing next-generation battery technology for e-bikes and light electric vehicles.

ev.headline: Vehicle-to-grid technology enables EV batteries to monetize grid services and demand response
ev.summary: Vehicle-to-grid (V2G) bidirectional charging is poised to transform fleet energy management by allowing EV batteries to provide demand response, frequency regulation, and ancillary grid services.

ev.headline: Sony Honda Mobility AFEELA 1 debuts in California 2026 with $102,900 Signature trim
ev.summary: Sony Honda Mobility's AFEELA 1 sedan launches in California in 2026 starting at $102,900 Signature trim, featuring Level 2+ autonomous driving and AI entertainment integration.

ev.headline: EV battery prices drop 8% since 2024; Goldman Sachs projects 50% decline by 2026
ev.summary: Lithium-ion battery pack pricing fell 8% to $108/kWh since 2024, with expectations battery costs could drop nearly 50% in 2026 compared to 2023 levels per Goldman Sachs.

ev.headline: Lucid plans robotaxi, targets $1 billion software revenue by decade's end
ev.summary: Lucid Motors revealed plans for a two-seat robotaxi and autonomy subscription service launching early 2027, targeting $1 billion in annual software and services revenue by late 2026.

ev.headline: Trump administration rolls back EV incentives, creates policy uncertainty for auto industry
ev.summary: The Trump administration rescinded federal EV tax credits, paused NEVI charging infrastructure funding, and weakened clean car standards, creating significant uncertainty for automakers' EV strategies.

ev.headline: China's electric vehicle makers gain global market share amid U.S. policy shifts
ev.summary: Chinese EV makers including BYD and others are expanding internationally and gaining market share as U.S. policy reversals reduce American automaker focus on electrification.
ev.source: Columbia SIPA Center on Global Energy Policy

ev.headline: EV supply chain decarbonization advances; Ford, Mercedes, Tesla lead clean manufacturing
ev.summary: Lead the Charge Leaderboard shows automakers accelerating EV supply chain decarbonization efforts, with Ford, Mercedes, Tesla, Volvo and Volkswagen pushing ahead in sustainable practices.

ev.headline: CATL begins production at $8.2 billion Hungary factory for BMW, Mercedes battery supply
ev.summary: Chinese battery giant CATL is ramping production at its new $8.2 billion Hungarian facility to supply batteries for BMW and Mercedes-Benz, expanding global battery manufacturing presence.

ev.headline: Nissan and Mitsubishi launch new EV based on redesigned 2026 Leaf platform
ev.summary: Nissan completely overhauled the Leaf for 2026 as a subcompact SUV while Mitsubishi announced a new EV based on the Leaf platform launching in second half 2026.

ev.headline: BYD plans global 1,500 kW Flash Charging network by late 2026 for 5-minute charging
ev.summary: BYD plans to deploy a global 1,500 kW 'Flash Charging' network by late 2026, enabling compatible EVs to charge to 70% capacity in just five minutes.

energy.headline: U.S. Solar Market Insight Report 2025 Year in Review Released
energy.summary: The U.S. solar industry installed 43.1 GW of capacity in 2025, marking a 14% decline from 2024 despite residential solar ending virtually flat. The industry navigated trade actions and tax credit reversals.

energy.headline: U.S. Energy Storage Industry Sets Record with 57.6 GWh of New Capacity in 2025
energy.summary: The energy storage sector installed a record-breaking 57.6 GWh in 2025, with residential storage growing 51% year-over-year. Virtual power plant programs in states are driving deployment.

energy.headline: Solar and Storage to Lead 86 GW of Record-Breaking New U.S. Capacity in 2026
energy.summary: Solar and storage combined will account for 79% of 86 GW of new capacity additions in 2026, marking the largest single-year capacity addition in two decades. California's residential battery attachment rates reach 69%.

energy.headline: California Appeals Court Upholds Net Metering 3.0 Reduction
energy.summary: A California appeals court upheld a 2022 decision to reduce rooftop solar payments by approximately 75%, backing utilities' position that solar owners should pay fair share of grid maintenance costs.

energy.headline: Appeals Court Upholds California's Net Metering 3.0
energy.summary: The California First Appellate District Court upheld NEM 3.0 tariff changes that substantially reduced utility compensation for customer-generated power, which had been linked to 77-85% drop in residential solar sales.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Installs Surged 205% Before Tax Credit Cut
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar systems before federal tax credits expired December 31, 2025, creating a 205% surge in demand and reshaping the home electrification market. Solar prices remained stable at $2.49 per watt.

energy.headline: US Residential Solar Enters Post-Incentive Era After ITC Expiry Surge
energy.summary: Following the One Big Beautiful Bill Act passage in July 2025, US residential solar entered a post-incentive phase with third-party ownership options like leases and PPAs becoming more popular. Financing flexibility now determines competitiveness.

energy.headline: EnergySage Report: Incentive Expiration Triggered Historic Market Shift
energy.summary: Battery attachment rates declined from 41% to 38% as homeowners prioritized solar incentives before deadline. National report reveals shift toward integrated home electrification beyond solar and storage.

energy.headline: Expiring Incentives Led to Record Demand in Residential Solar 2025
energy.summary: EnergySage reports 205% increase in homeowner engagement with solar installers in H2 2025 at $2.49 per watt median pricing. Battery attachment rates dropped in high-value markets like California (79% to 71%).

energy.headline: 600+ GWh of U.S. Energy Storage Expected by 2030
energy.summary: Despite federal actions targeting clean energy, over 600 GWh of battery storage is projected by 2030. Texas is set to overtake California as largest energy storage market in 2026.

energy.headline: Batteries Become the New Home Solar as Net Metering Evolves
energy.summary: California's NEM 3.0 has dramatically reduced export values for rooftop solar, making home batteries increasingly essential. Whole-home solutions combining solar, batteries, and EV charging are emerging.

energy.headline: California Residents Increasingly Pairing Battery Storage with Solar
energy.summary: Solar paired with battery installations comprise 9% of residential net metering capacity in California, with over 40,000 installations added between October 2023 and April 2024, accounting for 232 MW of storage.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries in 2026: Which Saves More on Solar?
energy.summary: Battery system costs range from $1,100-$1,300 per kWh in 2026, with typical 13.5 kWh systems exceeding $15,000 installed. 30% federal tax credit can reduce costs by $4,500 or more for qualifying systems.

energy.headline: Net Metering Still Necessary: Utility Debate Continues
energy.summary: Despite reduced net metering rates in many states, industry experts argue net metering remains valuable as batteries, EVs, and smart appliances change the grid landscape. 14 states have no net metering policies.

energy.headline: The 30% Solar Tax Credit is Dead—Long Live the 30% Solar Tax Credit
energy.summary: Section 25D residential tax credit expired December 31, 2025, but Section 48E clean electricity credit remains for lease/PPA structures. Homeowners can still access 30% incentives through alternative ownership models.

energy.headline: 2026-2027 U.S. Solar & HVAC Incentives Post-Federal Credit Era Analysis
energy.summary: States introduce new programs: California's heat pump rebates up to $4,250, Texas federal HOMES/HEAR programs allocated $690 million, Arizona's Efficiency Arizona launching. Home battery incentives critical for resilience.

energy.headline: Solar, Storage Account for 79% of New U.S. Power Capacity in 2025
energy.summary: U.S. installed 43 GW of solar capacity in 2025, maintaining position as largest source for fifth consecutive year. Solar and storage combined represented 79% of all new power capacity additions.

energy.headline: Nevada Boosts Battery Storage Capacity in 2025
energy.summary: Nevada deployed 1.4 GWh of additional battery storage in 2025 via Primergy's Gemini Solar project, raising state total to 6.3 GWh and ranking fourth nationally behind California, Texas, and Arizona.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Showcase Grid Benefits in 2025
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes in virtual power plant program dispatched 535 MW to grid during summer test, equivalent to half of San Francisco's energy demand, demonstrating distributed energy resource potential.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Home Battery Systems Comparison 2026
energy.summary: States are phasing out retail-rate net metering for discounted net billing. Full-retail net metering provides 1 kWh credit per 1 kWh exported; avoided-cost rates offer only 3-10 cents per kWh compared to 15-40 cent retail.

energy.headline: Energy Storage Market Outlook Q1 2026 Released
energy.summary: U.S. BESS deployments forecast to reach 70 GWh by 2026, with utility-scale accounting for 62.4 GWh and behind-the-meter markets 7.3 GWh. Quarterly publication tracks deployment, pricing, and policy landscape.

energy.headline: New U.S. Battery Capacity Forecast: 24.3 GW for 2026
energy.summary: Residential battery attachment rates surge due to net metering policy shifts. By 2030, analysts project one in eight American homes will have solar with majority choosing storage for maximum self-consumption.

v2x.headline: Xos to roll out V2G capability across full commercial EV lineup beginning April 2026
v2x.summary: Commercial EV builder Xos announced V2G production starting April 2026 on electric school bus platforms, with plans to expand bidirectional capability across entire portfolio including step vans and energy storage solutions.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils New IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy announced its new IQ Bidirectional EV Charger architecture enabling vehicle-to-home and vehicle-to-grid capabilities with DC charging, AI-powered energy management, and support for global standards.

v2x.headline: Yes, an EV really CAN power your home – if it's one of these
v2x.summary: Article updates which EV models support V2H functionality in 2026, including Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla Cybertruck, GM EVs, Nissan LEAF, Volvo EX90, and others with bidirectional charging capabilities.

v2x.headline: Toyota's Vehicle-to-Grid Program with Oncor demonstrates benefits of bidirectional BEV charging
v2x.summary: Toyota advanced its V2G pilot with Texas utility Oncor, demonstrating grid stability enhancement, backup power capability, and cost savings through bidirectional energy flow optimization.

v2x.headline: EV battery industry sees shift toward energy storage as EV demand softens
v2x.summary: US battery manufacturers are pivoting to energy storage production due to waning EV demand, with LG and SK Battery America retooling for stationary storage applications at existing facilities.

v2x.headline: Used electric vehicle batteries bolstering Texas power grid
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions deployed first 24 MWh battery project in Texas using second-life EV batteries for grid storage, with plans to expand to 100 MWh capacity across the state.

v2x.headline: Top 10 EV Battery Trends: V2G integration and ultra-fast charging lead 2026
v2x.summary: CALSTART identified V2G as major trend for 2026, highlighting machine learning integration, thermal management, battery cost reductions, and second-life battery applications for grid support.

v2x.headline: The Battery on Wheels Revolution: 2026 marks the dawn of $5.8B V2G era
v2x.summary: Global bidirectional EV charging market projected to reach $5.8 billion by 2036, with 2026 identified as critical 'takeoff point' for mass smart grid integration and distributed energy resources.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Home charging can cut costs and emissions across the USA
v2x.summary: New Nature Energy study finds V2H charging could save EV owners $2,400-$5,600 over vehicle lifetime while reducing emissions by 70-250% from household energy use.

v2x.headline: EV charging industry faces grid interconnection delays in 2026
v2x.summary: Battery storage projects linked to EV charging infrastructure are encountering extended grid interconnection timelines across US regions due to queue backlogs and procedural bottlenecks.

v2x.headline: V2G Industry enters transformative period with AI-driven energy management
v2x.summary: 2026 EV charging industry expects ultra-fast 350kW+ charging, V2G-enabled virtual power plants, and AI-driven energy management systems improving grid reliability and operator profitability.

v2x.headline: UC Irvine research: Vehicle-to-home charging technology launches for mass market
v2x.summary: Six California homes deployed Wallbox Quasar 2 bidirectional chargers with Kia EV9 vehicles for two-year real-world evaluation, demonstrating V2H grid-tied and off-grid capabilities.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz to introduce bidirectional charging for 2026 with new GLC
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz announced V2G capability coming to 2026 models starting with new electric GLC, partnering with Mobility House Energy for Europe-wide smart charging solutions rollout.

v2x.headline: V2G technology faces standardization challenges as AC/DC battle rages
v2x.summary: CleanTechnica analysis reveals competing bidirectional charging standards between DC and AC implementations are slowing V2G mass market deployment despite €100B EU grid cost savings potential.

v2x.headline: CES 2026: Autel Energy launches new EV charging infrastructure
v2x.summary: At CES 2026, Autel Energy unveiled latest AC and DC charging solutions including Avant family products supporting advanced EV charging and grid integration capabilities.

v2x.headline: What's next for EV batteries in 2026: Sodium-ion and Chinese manufacturing expansion
v2x.summary: MIT Technology Review examines 2026 EV battery trends including sodium-ion alternatives, LFP stationary storage production, and geopolitical shifts in battery supply chains.

v2x.headline: Kia EV9 owners gain V2H capabilities with Wallbox Quasar 2 charger pre-orders
v2x.summary: Kia announced V2H availability for EV9 owners through Wallbox's Quasar 2 bidirectional charger at $6,440, enabling home backup power and cost savings on electricity bills.

v2x.headline: V2G charging standards must include ISO 15118-20 and OCPP compliance by 2026
v2x.summary: Versinetic analysis shows OCPP compliance is becoming absolute requirement in 2026, with 250+ EV models expected requiring standardized interoperability for bidirectional charging deployment.

v2x.headline: Five electromobility trends shaping EV charging in 2026: V2G integration accelerates
v2x.summary: Ekoenergetyka highlights V2G and V2H technologies increasingly integrated into charging infrastructure, with EVs functioning as dynamic energy storage assets supporting grid stability.

v2x.headline: New vehicle-to-grid study brings Australia's rollout closer in 2026
v2x.summary: CSIRO and Essential Energy demonstrated CCS2 bidirectional charging with Ford F-150 Lightning and Nissan Leaf, proving commercial V2G feasibility for Australian households and grid support.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid market projected to grow from $11.4B to $129.8B by 2034
v2x.summary: Global V2G market valued at £11.4 billion in 2024 projected to reach £129.8 billion by 2034, representing 27% annual growth with Tesla and GM scaling V2G-capable vehicle production.

v2x.headline: V2G market to grow from $14M in 2024 to $117M by 2032
v2x.summary: Driivz market analysis shows V2G market expanding at 30% CAGR through 2032, with early adopters positioned to benefit most from rapidly emerging bidirectional charging opportunities.

v2x.headline: GM Energy V2H system dominates market with seven compatible Ultium-based EVs
v2x.summary: GM offers broadest V2H compatibility across Cadillac, Chevrolet, and GMC brands with PowerShift Charger and V2H Enablement Kit, now exploring leasing programs to increase accessibility.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV charging in 2026: Which cars support V2G and V2H technology
v2x.summary: EcoFlow comprehensive guide lists 2026 EVs supporting bidirectional charging including Ford F-150 Lightning, Tesla, GM Ultium platform, Mitsubishi, and Volvo with varying V2G/V2H capabilities.

v2x.headline: Nature study on V2G potential in China's megacities reveals 0-12% peak demand reduction
v2x.summary: Nature Communications research demonstrates vehicle-to-grid load shifting potential in Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, and Shenzhen considering real-world user behavior and fast charging deployment.
